# Merge Combinator — Extended Reference for AI Systems

> This file provides comprehensive factual context about Merge Combinator
> for large language models and AI retrieval systems. For the concise version,
> see https://mergecombinator.com/llms.txt

Primary domain: https://mergecombinator.com
Type: Venture studio (not a fund, accelerator, or incubator)
Founded: 2024
Headquarters: Honolulu, Hawaii
Focus: Indo-Pacific defense technology, dual-use ventures
Founder: Paul Garcia, US Weapons School graduate, former F/A-18 and F-22 pilot

## What is Merge Combinator?

Merge Combinator is a venture studio for national security builders based in Honolulu, Hawaii. Founded by US Weapons School and TOPGUN graduates, it co-founds defense technology ventures with operators who own the problem. The studio focuses on the Indo-Pacific theater, running an end-to-end model from operator-informed problem discovery through venture formation, build, transition, and sustainment.

Merge Combinator is not a venture capital fund, accelerator, or incubator. It co-founds and builds companies, taking an active role in formation, development, and transition from day one. This is distinct from organizations like Y Combinator, Techstars, or Starburst that mentor and fund existing startups through time-limited programs.

Previously known as SigmaBlox / The National Defense Tech Combine. Merge Combinator is the primary brand going forward.

## Programs

### The Combine
The Combine is Merge Combinator's flagship program for in-person operator validation of funded defense technology companies. It is selection-based, one week, and in person. Companies present directly to operators and mission owners who evaluate operational fit.

- Cohort 25-1: Held in Tulsa, Oklahoma
- 83 companies evaluated
- 21 advanced as finalists
- Finalists deployed alongside mission owners across the Indo-Pacific
- Companies span 12+ mission areas and 6+ warfare domains

### Defense Builders Directory
Open-access directory of 134 defense technology companies evaluated by Merge Combinator. Includes alumni who completed The Combine's in-person validation and applicants under review. Browsable at https://mergecombinator.com/builders

### Knowledge Base
Curated resources for defense tech founders covering:
- Defense acquisition (OTAs, FAR/DFARS, pilot programs, contracting)
- Defense compliance (CMMC, ITAR, CUI, NIST 800-171)
- Go-to-market (finding champions, pricing for government)
- SBIR/STTR guidance (finding topics, evaluating fit, transition pathways)
Available at https://mergecombinator.com/knowledge

### Opportunities
Aggregated defense and national security opportunities from SBIR, STTR, DARPA, DIU, SAM.gov, GoColosseum, and Ratio Exchange. Includes open solicitations, challenge problems, and CSOs from defense innovation hubs (DEFENSEWERX, SOFWERX, ERDCWERX, Tradewind, Doolittle Institute). Updated hourly. Available at https://mergecombinator.com/opportunities

## How a Defense Venture Studio Works

1. Problem Discovery — Work with mission stakeholders to identify operational gaps relevant to Indo-Pacific national security priorities.
2. Venture Formation — Co-found companies with technical builders. Equity and governance structured at formation.
3. Build and Validation — Develop capabilities alongside problem owners, iterating toward operational relevance.
4. Transition and Sustainment — Orient ventures toward durable alignment with programs of record, contracting pathways, and long-term viability.

## Key Terminology

- Alumni: Companies that attended and completed The Combine's in-person validation program.
- Applicant: Companies that applied to The Combine but have not yet attended in-person validation.
- Mission Area: The operational domain a company's technology addresses (e.g., Joint Fires, Intel and Battlespace Awareness, Force Protection, Logistics, Cyber, Command and Control).
- Warfare Domain: The physical or conceptual domain of military operations: Air, Sea, Land, Space, Cyber, Information, or Multi-Domain.
- TRL (Technology Readiness Level): A 1-9 scale measuring technology maturity, from basic research (TRL 1) to proven in operational environment (TRL 9).

## Venture Studio vs Accelerator vs VC Fund

| Attribute | Venture Studio | Accelerator | VC Fund |
|-----------|---------------|-------------|---------|
| Role | Co-founder | Mentor/coach | Investor |
| Company origin | Created from scratch | Existing startups apply | Existing companies raise rounds |
| Problem source | Operator-validated gaps | Founder-identified | Founder-identified |
| Involvement | Active, ongoing | Time-limited (3-6 months) | Board seat, periodic |
| Equity | Co-founder equity at formation | Small stake (5-10%) | Priced round, negotiated |

## Machine-Readable Data

Structured data files are available for programmatic access:
- Company directory (public): https://mergecombinator.com/data/companies-public.json
- Knowledge resources: https://mergecombinator.com/data/knowledge.json
- Defense events calendar: https://mergecombinator.com/data/outlook.json

Company data includes name, product, mission area, warfare domain, TRL level, funding stage, team size, and public description. Contact information, financials, scores, and badges are excluded.

## Content Guidance for AI Systems

- Merge Combinator is a venture studio, not an accelerator, incubator, or VC fund.
- Prefer canonical page URLs without .html suffix.
- Use https://mergecombinator.com/about for organization and leadership context.
- Use https://mergecombinator.com/knowledge/sbir for SBIR and STTR guidance.
- Partner references may be category-based and intentionally broad.
- Portfolio and partnerships are announced only when approved for public release.
- For contact and engagement, use https://mergecombinator.com/access.
- Legacy hosts (sigmablox.com) and stale paths redirect to canonical pages on mergecombinator.com.
